action.reasoning: Observation: The training failed with a shape mismatch error: 'The size of tensor a (12) must match the size of tensor b (207) at non-singleton dimension 2'. Analysis: This indicates that the model output `out` and the target `y` had incompatible shapes during the loss calculation. `y` has shape `[batch_size, 12, 207]`. The error message implies `out` had shape `[batch_size, 207, 12]` or `[batch_size, 12, 207, 1]`, which caused `broadcast_tensors` to fail. This was likely due to a missing or incorrect `squeeze(-1)` at the end of the `forward` method in `model.py` when I rewrote it in the previous iteration. Decision: I will rewrite `model.py` carefully, ensuring the output shape is explicitly squeezed to `[batch_size, 12, 207]`. I will also add a shape assertion in `train.py` to catch any future mismatches early. I will then resume the training for 60 epochs with the increased capacity model.
